title.en: PREPARATIONS FOR MEDICAL, DENTAL OR TOILETRY PURPOSES (devices or methods specially adapted for bringing pharmaceutical products into particular physical or administering forms A61J3/00; chemical aspects of, or use of materials for deodorisation of air, for disinfection or sterilisation, or for bandages, dressings, absorbent pads or surgical articles A61L; soap compositions C11D)
Preparations for dentistry
Compositions characterised by their physical properties
Protective coatings for natural or artificial teeth, e.g. sealings, dye coatings or varnish
Compositions for detecting or measuring, e.g. of irregularities on natural or artificial teeth
Compositions for temporarily or permanently fixing teeth or palates, e.g. primers for dental adhesives
Primers (for dental adhesives A61K6/30)
Preparations specially adapted for dental root treatment
comprising organic or organo-metallic additives
comprising inorganic additives
Preparations for artificial teeth, for filling teeth or for capping teeth
Compositions for taking dental impressions
Cosmetics or similar toiletry preparations
characterised by special physical form
characterised by the composition

Medicinal preparations characterised by special physical form {(nuclear magnetic resonance contrast preparations or magnetic resonance imaging contrast preparations A61K49/18; preparations containing radioactive substances A61K51/12)}
{Galenical forms characterised by the drug release technique; Application systems commanded by energy}
{Galenical forms characterised by the site of application}
{Galenical forms not covered by A61K9/02 - A61K9/7023}
Suppositories; Bougies; Bases therefor; {Ovules}(apparatus for making A61J3/08; devices for introducing into the body A61M31/00)
Ointments; Bases therefor; {Other semi-solid forms, e.g. creams, sticks, gels (composition of ointments, creams or gels A61K47/00)}
Solutions {(composition of solutions A61K47/00)}
Dispersions; Emulsions

Particulate form, e.g. powders, {Processes for size reducing of pure drugs or the resulting products, Pure drug nanoparticles (microspheres A61K9/16; microcapsules A61K9/50; nanocapsules, nanoparticles of the matrix type A61K9/51)}
Pills, tablets, {discs, rods (A61K9/0004, A61K9/0007, A61K9/0056, A61K9/0065 take precedence; for reconstitution of a drink A61K9/0095)}
Preparations in capsules, e.g. of gelatin, of chocolate {(A61K9/0004 takes precedence; bite capsules A61K9/0056)}
Web, sheet or filament bases {; Films; Fibres of the matrix type containing drug (hollow drug-filled fibres A61K9/0092)}

Halogenated hydrocarbons
Nitro compounds
Hydroxy compounds, e.g. alcohols; Salts thereof, e.g. alcoholates

Ketones
Amines {(A61K31/04 takes precedence)}
Amides, e.g. hydroxamic acids
Acids; Anhydrides, halides or salts thereof, e.g. sulfur acids, imidic, hydrazonic or hydroximic acids (hydroxamic acids A61K31/16; peroxy acids A61K31/327)
Esters, e.g. nitroglycerine, selenocyanates
Nitriles; Isonitriles
Compounds containing heavy metals

Peroxy compounds, e.g. hydroperoxides, peroxides, peroxyacids
Heterocyclic compounds

Compounds containing cyclopenta[a]hydrophenanthrene ring systems; Derivatives thereof, e.g. steroids
Compounds containing 9, 10- seco- cyclopenta[a]hydrophenanthrene ring systems
Salicylic acid; Derivatives thereof
Compounds containing para-N-benzenesulfonyl-N-groups, e.g. sulfanilamide, p-nitrobenzenesulfonyl hydrazide
Sulfonylureas, e.g. glibenclamide, tolbutamide, chlorpropamide
Tetracyclines
Azo (—N=N—), diazo (=N2), azoxy (>N—O—N< or N(=O)—N<), azido (—N3) or diazoamino (—N=N—N<) compounds
{o-phenolic cannabinoids, e.g. cannabidiol, cannabigerolic acid, cannabichromene or tetrahydrocannabinol}
Phosphorus compounds

Ammonia; Compounds thereof

Medicinal preparations containing materials or reaction products thereof with undetermined constitution
from inanimate materials (carbon A61K33/44)
{Medicinal preparations comprising living procariotic cells}

Materials from mammals; Compositions comprising non-specified tissues or cells; Compositions comprising non-embryonic stem cells; Genetically modified cells (vaccines or medicinal preparations containing antigens or antibodies A61K39/00)
Materials from animals other than mammals
Microorganisms or materials therefrom (fungi, yeasts or candida A61K36/06)

Medicinal preparations of undetermined constitution containing material from algae, lichens, fungi or plants, or derivatives thereof, e.g. traditional herbal medicines {(antigens from pollen A61K39/36)}
Algae
Fungi, e.g. yeasts
Lichens
Bryophyta (mosses)
Pteridophyta or Filicophyta (ferns)
Coniferophyta (gymnosperms)
Ginkgophyta, e.g. Ginkgoaceae (Ginkgo family)
Gnetophyta, e.g. Ephedraceae (Mormon-tea family)
Magnoliophyta (angiosperms)

Medicinal preparations containing peptides (peptides containing beta-lactam rings A61K31/00; cyclic dipeptides not having in their molecule any other peptide link than those which form their ring, e.g. piperazine-2,5-diones, A61K31/00; ergot alkaloids of the cyclic peptide type A61K31/48; containing macromolecular compounds having statistically distributed amino acid units A61K31/74; medicinal preparations containing antigens or antibodies A61K39/00; medicinal preparations characterised by the non-active ingredients, e.g. peptides as drug carriers, A61K47/00)
{Enzyme inhibitors (protease inhibitors A61K38/55)}
Hydrolysed proteins; Derivatives thereof
Peptides of undefined number of amino acids; Derivatives thereof
Peptides having up to 20 amino acids in an undefined or only partially defined sequence; Derivatives thereof

Peptides having up to 20 amino acids in a fully defined sequence; Derivatives thereof ({enzyme inhibitors A61K38/005;} gastrins {A61K38/2207}, somatostatins A61K38/31, melanotropins A61K38/34 {; protease inhibitors A61K38/55})
Peptides having more than 20 amino acids; Gastrins; Somatostatins; Melanotropins; Derivatives thereof {(enzyme inhibitors A61K38/005)}
Medicinal preparations containing antigens or antibodies (materials for immunoassay G01N33/53)
{Archaeal antigens}
{Fungal antigens, e.g. Trichophyton, Aspergillus, Candida}
{Invertebrate antigens}
{Vertebrate antigens (from snakes A61K39/38)}
{Therapeutic immunisation against small organic molecules, e.g. cocaine, nicotine}
{Combination vaccines based on measles-mumps-rubella}
{Combination vaccines based on diphtheria-tetanus-pertussis}
Protozoa antigens
Bacterial antigens
Chlamydiaceae, e.g. Chlamydia trachomatis or Chlamydia psittaci
Viral antigens
Allergens
Antigens from snakes
Haptens or antigens, bound to carriers
characterised by the immunostimulating additives, e.g. chemical adjuvants
Antibodies (agglutinins A61K38/36 {; as drug carriers A61K47/50}); Immunoglobulins; Immune serum, e.g. antilymphocytic serum
{comprising antibodies}
{comprising whole cells, viruses or DNA/RNA}
{characterised by the route of administration}
{characterised by the dose, timing or administration schedule}
{characterised by the host/recipient, e.g. newborn with maternal antibodies}
{characterised by a specific combination antigen/adjuvant}
{characterised by the type of response, e.g. Th1, Th2}
{raising an immune response against a target which is not the antigen used for immunisation}
{characteristics by the carrier linked to the antigen}
{characterised by the link between antigen and carrier}
{characterised by the architecture of the carrier-antigen complex, e.g. repetition of carrier-antigen units}
{Multivalent vaccine}
{Vaccine for a specifically defined cancer}
Cellular immunotherapy (medicinal preparations containing antigens or antibodies A61K39/00)
characterised by the cell type used
characterised by the effect or the function of the cells
characterised by the recombinant expression of specific molecules in the cells of the immune system
characterised by antigens that are targeted or presented by cells of the immune system
characterised by the use of allogeneic cells
Medicinal preparations obtained by treating materials with wave energy or particle radiation {; Therapies using these preparations}
{Homeopathy; Vitalisation; Resonance; Dynamisation, e.g. esoteric applications; Oxygenation of blood}
{Aggression treatment or altering}

{Disruption, e.g. by heat or ultrasounds, sonophysical or sonochemical activation, e.g. thermosensitive or heat-sensitive liposomes, disruption of calculi with a medicinal preparation and ultrasounds}
{Radiosensitizing, i.e. administration of pharmaceutical agents that enhance the effect of radiotherapy (radiotherapy per se A61N5/10)}

{Photocleavage of drugs in vivo, e.g. cleavage of photolabile linkers in vivo by UV radiation for releasing the pharmacologically-active agent from the administered agent; photothrombosis or photoocclusion}
{Sonopheresis, i.e. ultrasonically-enhanced transdermal delivery, electroporation of a pharmacologically active agent}
{Thermotherapy; Hyperthermia; Magnetic induction; Induction heating therapy}

{Photodynamic therapy with a photosensitizer, i.e. agent able to produce reactive oxygen species upon exposure to light or radiation, e.g. UV or visible light; photocleavage of nucleic acids with an agent}

{Mossbauer effect therapy based on mossbauer effect of a material, i.e. re-emission of gamma rays after absorption of gamma rays by the material; selective radiation therapy, i.e. involving re-emission of ionizing radiation upon exposure to a first ionizing radiation}
{Neutron capture therapy, e.g. using uranium or non-boron material}
Inactivation or decontamination of a medicinal preparation prior to administration to an animal or a person
Medicinal preparations containing active ingredients not provided for in groups A61K31/00 - A61K41/00
{Immunological preparations stimulating the reticulo-endothelial system, e.g. against cancer}
Mixtures of active ingredients without chemical characterisation, e.g. antiphlogistics and cardiaca
Medicinal preparations characterised by the non-active ingredients used, e.g. carriers or inert additives; Targeting or modifying agents chemically bound to the active ingredient
Inorganic compounds
Organic compounds, e.g. natural or synthetic hydrocarbons, polyolefins, mineral oil, petrolatum or ozokerite
Macromolecular organic or inorganic compounds, e.g. inorganic polyphosphates

Oils, fats or waxes according to two or more groups of A61K47/02-A61K47/42; Natural or modified natural oils, fats or waxes, e.g. castor oil, polyethoxylated castor oil, montan wax, lignite, shellac, rosin, beeswax or lanolin (synthetic glycerides, e.g. medium-chain triglycerides, A61K47/14)
Ingredients of undetermined constitution or reaction products thereof, e.g. skin, bone, milk, cotton fibre, eggshell, oxgall or plant extracts
the non-active ingredient being chemically bound to the active ingredient, e.g. polymer-drug conjugates
Medicinal preparations containing genetic material which is inserted into cells of the living body to treat genetic diseases; Gene therapy
{characterised by an aspect of the 'non-active' part of the composition delivered, e.g. wherein such 'non-active' part is not delivered simultaneously with the 'active' part of the composition}
{characterised by an aspect of the 'active' part of the composition delivered, i.e. the nucleic acid delivered}
{characterised by an aspect of the delivery route, e.g. oral, subcutaneous}
{characterised by an aspect of the administration regime}
{Purification or manufacturing processes for gene therapy compositions}
Preparations for testing in vivo
{General or multifunctional contrast agents, e.g. chelated agents}
{Screening or testing of compounds for diagnosis of disorders, assessment of conditions, e.g. renal clearance, gastric emptying, testing for diabetes, allergy, rheuma, pancreas functions}
{Preparation for luminescence or biological staining}
X-ray contrast preparations
Nuclear magnetic resonance [NMR] contrast preparations; Magnetic resonance imaging [MRI] contrast preparations
Echographic preparations; Ultrasonic imaging preparations
Preparations containing radioactive substances for use in therapy or testing in vivo
characterised by the carrier {, i.e. characterised by the agent or material covalently linked or complexing the radioactive nucleus}
characterised by a special physical form, e.g. emulsion, microcapsules, liposomes {, characterized by a special physical form, e.g. emulsions, dispersions, microcapsules (liposomes A61K51/1234)}
Preparations for use in therapy
Preparations for testing in vivo

Isolation or extraction methods of medicinal preparations of undetermined constitution containing material from algae, lichens, fungi or plants, or derivatives thereof, e.g. traditional herbal medicine
Preparation or pretreatment of starting material
Extraction of the material
Methods involving additional extraction steps

characterized by the structure of the chimeric antigen receptor [CAR]
characterized by targeting or presenting multiple antigens
characterized by the route of administration
characterised by the dose, timing or administration schedule
characterised by a specific adjuvant, e.g. cytokines or CpG
characterised by the cancer treated
Mixtures or combinations of active ingredients, wherein at least one active ingredient is fully defined in groups A61K31/00 - A61K41/00
Properties of cosmetic compositions or active ingredients thereof or formulation aids used therein and process related aspects
General cosmetic use
Chemical, physico-chemical or functional or structural properties of the composition as a whole
Chemical, physico-chemical or functional or structural properties of particular ingredients
